title: Haddy the Mouse and the Lego Airplane
summary: When young Izzy the Kiddo finds herself stuck in bed with a cold, her friend Haddy the Mouse pays a visit to cheer her up. Rejecting soup in favor of creativity, the pair dives into a world of building blocks. Joined by a Lego architect named Uncle Bill, they construct elaborate symmetrical stairs and a soaring airplane, proving that a little imagination is the best medicine for any illness.

highlightQuote.text: Haddy, be very quiet. I'm sick and I'm resting, so you better be very, very, very quiet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Haddy, be very quiet. I'm sick and I'm resting, so you better be very, very, very quiet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Izzy the kiddo was sick and she was resting, and Haddy the Mouse came to visit her. Haddy the mouse said, 'Oh hello Izzy, I heard you're sick. How are you feeling?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm good, but I'm a little sick. So I'm resting in bed and I'm going... Achoo! Achoo! I'm sick.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Haddy said 'Gesundheit! Here's a tissue.' And Haddy said, 'Is there anything I can bring you? Would you like, um, some soup?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I just need to play with some Legos.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh some Legos! I'll bring you some Legos. And so Haddy went to her house and got a big box of Legos and she brought them to Izzy the Kiddo. 'Izzy!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And Izzy said, 'I already have some Legos which I can build with.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. And Haddy the Mouse said, 'Can I play Legos with you, Izzy?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Of course. What should we build together?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What should we build?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, first I have to go build with Uncle Bill, Uncle Bill, Uncle, Uncle, Uncle Bill. Uncle... Uncle Bill. Daddy can you help me find Uncle Bill?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. Oh, I see him, he's right here.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uncle Bill, what do you work on today?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, well, I'm working on building something very special.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But I'm going to build with Legos too.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh you are? I'm going to build some stairs.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm building stairs too.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Little brick.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so, even though Izzy the Kiddo was feeling sick, she had a good time building with Legos with her friend Haddy the Mouse. And what did they build together? What did they make?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Uncle Bill says, 'Aha! Here's my thing that I made. What do you think of that?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I like it, Uncle Bill.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Thank you. It's symmetrical. It's got stairs on both sides.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Uncle Bill, look what I'm making. I made a little airplane for you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow! What a cool airplane. Thanks for making that, Izzy. And what did Haddy the Mouse build?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Haddy-ma, look at your airplane, for you and Uncle Bill. Wow what a cool airplane.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Izzy, can you make it fly?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait, I'll attach the bottom. See how it flies and flies way up high into the sky.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, it's flying high up in the sky. Oh, and that's our timer. So that's the end of Stars and Stories and Lego School. And Haddy the Mouse said, 'Bye bye, Izzy, I hope you feel better.' And that's the end.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, let's tuck in our Legos again.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Why?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Cause it's time for your nap.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Well, I'll tuck in my airplane.

characters.name: Izzy the Kiddo
characters.description: Once a quiet dreamer sketching from a recovery bed, this brave explorer now leads her companions through Hattie’s workshop and the lightless abyss, guided by a beacon of special light. Clad in a rugged driving suit alongside Bluey and Fincat’s Mouse, she wields heavy hammers and a magic wand with logistical precision inherited from her Nana. From trading plastic bricks for rare mushrooms to orchestrating neighborhood cleanups, she balances specialized gear with a playful spirit. Choosing the spark of a game over the gravity of a traditional quest, she ensures her radiant creativity and guiding light remain her most powerful tools for navigating the unknown.

characters.name: Haddy the Mouse
characters.description: A master hat maker residing in a hollow tree, this creative mouse weaves shimmering masterpieces with a vibrant imagination and a nurturing heart. Whether she is brightening Izzy’s door with a heavy box of building blocks or tenderly watching over her baby sister, she moves with a grace that even a brief, surreal transformation into a mushroom could not dampen. A devoted friend and artisan, she remains a beacon of light, stitching care and sparkle into the world one whimsical creation at a time.

characters.name: Uncle Bill
characters.description: A diligent crew member and master Lego architect, this former visitor to Balloon World crafts perfectly symmetrical stairways and shapes iridescent purple slime into glistening rocket ships. Renowned for a sharp eye that once pierced Pluto’s crystalline shadows, they recently turned their gaze toward the forest’s depths, acting as a compassionate friend to discover and tend to a sick creature hidden in the brush. Whether providing blocks for builders, weaving bedtime stories, or offering woodland mercy, they remain a helpful soul. Yet, between these moments of structural precision and quiet rescue, they harbor a penchant for deep rest, often found napping peacefully inside the colorful confines of a toy container, tucked away amidst the playthings they share.

places.name: Izzy's Bed
places.description: The cozy spot where Izzy rests and builds toys while recovering from her cold.

places.name: Haddy's House
places.description: The home of Haddy the Mouse where she keeps her large collection of Legos.
